attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# jsprit
2	
3	jsprit is a java based, open source toolkit for solving rich [Traveling Salesman Problems (TSP)](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Travelling_salesman_problem) and [Vehicle Routing Problems (VRP)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vehicle_routing_problem).
4	
5	It is lightweight, flexible and easy-to-use, and based on a single all-purpose [meta-heuristic](docs/Meta-Heuristic.md) currently solving:
6	
7	- Capacitated VRP
8	- Multiple Depot VRP
9	- VRP with Time Windows
10	- VRP with Backhauls
11	- VRP with Pickups and Deliveries
12	- VRP with Heterogeneous Fleet
13	- Time-dependent VRP
14	- Traveling Salesman Problem
15	- Dial-a-Ride Problem
16	- Various combinations of these types
17	
18	Setting up the problem, defining additional constraints, modifying the algorithms and visualising the discovered solutions is straightforward. It is designed for change and extension with a modular architecture and comprehensive test coverage. [More features...](https://github.com/graphhopper/jsprit/blob/master/docs/Features.textile)
19	
20	The jsprit-project is maintained by [GraphHopper](https://graphhopper.com/).
21	
22	## Requirements
23	
24	- **Java 21** or higher
25	
26	## Installation
27	
28	**Maven:**
29	```xml
30	<dependency>
31	    <groupId>com.graphhopper</groupId>
32	    <artifactId>jsprit-core</artifactId>
33	    <version>2.0.0</version>
34	</dependency>
35	```
36	
37	**Gradle:**
38	```groovy
39	implementation 'com.graphhopper:jsprit-core:2.0.0'
40	```
41	
42	## Quick Start
43	
44	```java
45	// Define vehicles
46	VehicleType vehicleType = VehicleTypeImpl.Builder.newInstance("type")
47	    .addCapacityDimension(0, 10)
48	    .build();
49	
50	VehicleImpl vehicle = VehicleImpl.Builder.newInstance("vehicle")
51	    .setStartLocation(Location.newInstance(0, 0))
52	    .setType(vehicleType)
53	    .build();
54	
55	// Define jobs
56	Service job1 = Service.Builder.newInstance("job1")
57	    .addSizeDimension(0, 1)
58	    .setLocation(Location.newInstance(5, 7))
59	    .build();
60	
61	Service job2 = Service.Builder.newInstance("job2")
62	    .addSizeDimension(0, 2)
63	    .setLocation(Location.newInstance(3, 4))
64	    .build();
65	
66	// Build the problem
67	VehicleRoutingProblem vrp = VehicleRoutingProblem.Builder.newInstance()
68	    .addVehicle(vehicle)
69	    .addJob(job1)
70	    .addJob(job2)
71	    .build();
72	
73	// Solve
74	VehicleRoutingAlgorithm algorithm = Jsprit.createAlgorithm(vrp);
75	Collection<VehicleRoutingProblemSolution> solutions = algorithm.searchSolutions();
76	VehicleRoutingProblemSolution best = Solutions.bestOf(solutions);
77	```
78	
79	## What's New in 2.0
80	
81	**Independent Operator Selection** - Configure ruin and insertion strategies separately:
82	```java
83	VehicleRoutingAlgorithm algorithm = Jsprit.Builder.newInstance(vrp)
84	    .addRuinOperator(0.4, Ruin.radial())
85	    .addRuinOperator(0.4, Ruin.random())
86	    .addRuinOperator(0.2, Ruin.worst())
87	    .addInsertionOperator(0.7, Insertion.regretFast())
88	    .addInsertionOperator(0.3, Insertion.best())
89	    .buildAlgorithm();
90	```
91	
92	**Algorithm Event System** - Monitor algorithm execution:
93	```java
94	AlgorithmEventAdapter adapter = new AlgorithmEventAdapter(algorithm);
95	algorithm.addListener(adapter);
96	algorithm.addEventListener(event -> {
97	    if (event instanceof StrategyExecuted e) {
98	        System.out.println("Strategy: " + e.strategyId());
99	    }
100	});
101	```
102	
103	**Other improvements:**
104	- Regret-k insertion (configurable k)
105	- MST-based cluster ruin strategy
106	- Declarative initial solutions with SolutionSpec
107	- Job.Type enum for cleaner type handling
108	
109	See [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) for full details.
110	
111	Upgrading from 1.x? See [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md).
112	
113	## Documentation
114	
115	Please visit [docs](https://github.com/graphhopper/jsprit/blob/master/docs/Home.md) to learn more.
116	
117	## Modules and Dependencies
118	
119	Please read [NOTICE.md](https://github.com/graphhopper/jsprit/blob/master/NOTICE.md) to get to know the direct dependencies of each module.
120	
121	## License
122	
123	This software is released under [Apache License v2](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0).
124	
125	## Contribution
126	
127	Any contribution is welcome. Feel free to improve jsprit and make pull requests. If you want to contribute to jsprit, fork the project and build your fork, make changes, run tests and make a pull request (see [GitHub help](https://help.github.com/articles/fork-a-repo) for details).
128	
129	See who has contributed [here](https://github.com/graphhopper/jsprit/graphs/contributors).
130	
131	## Contact
132	
133	**Forum:** In the [GraphHopper Forum](https://discuss.graphhopper.com/) you can discuss jsprit related issues and get answers to your questions.
134	
135	**Issue Tracker:** For bugs and feature requests, use the [issue tracker](https://github.com/graphhopper/jsprit/issues).
136	
137	**Email:** If you prefer private communication, [contact us via mail](https://graphhopper.com/#contact).
138

## Jsprit Project Exploration Report

### 1. Overall Project Structure

**Project**: jsprit - A Java-based Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) solver  
**Version**: 2.1.0-SNAPSHOT  
**Maintained by**: GraphHopper  
**License**: Apache License v2  
**Build System**: Maven  
**Java Version**: 21+

**Module Structure**:
```
jsprit (parent POM)
├── jsprit-core (344 Java files + 137 test files)
└── jsprit-analysis (9 Java files)
```

### 2. Project Purpose

jsprit is a lightweight, flexible Java toolkit for solving rich Vehicle Routing Problems (VRP) and Traveling Salesman Problems (TSP). It supports:

- Capacitated VRP
- Multiple Depot VRP
- VRP with Time Windows
- VRP with Backhauls
- VRP with Pickups and Deliveries
- VRP with Heterogeneous Fleet
- Time-dependent VRP
- Traveling Salesman Problem
- Dial-a-Ride Problem
- Various problem combinations

The core library uses a single all-purpose meta-heuristic algorithm based on ruin-and-recreate approach.

### 3. Key Architectural Patterns

#### Core Domain Model Interfaces:
- **Vehicle** (`Vehicle.java`) - Extends `HasId`, `HasIndex`; represents vehicle with departure/arrival times, type, location, skills, breaks
- **VehicleType** (`VehicleType.java`) - Defines vehicle characteristics
- **Job** (`Job.java`) - Extends `HasId`, `HasIndex`; includes enum `Type` with values: SHIPMENT, SERVICE, PICKUP_SERVICE, DELIVERY_SERVICE, BREAK_SERVICE, EN_ROUTE_DELIVERY, EN_ROUTE_PICKUP
- **VehicleRoute** - Solution container for routes
- **TourActivity** - Represents activities in routes (Start, End, ServiceActivity, PickupService, DeliverService, etc.)
- **Location** - Coordinate system for jobs and vehicles
- **Capacity** - Multi-dimensional capacity handling
- **Skills** - Required skills for jobs/vehicles

#### Algorithm Interfaces:
- **VehicleRoutingAlgorithm** - Main solver orchestrator with listener pattern support
- **RuinStrategy** - Destroys current solution (ruin operators)
- **InsertionStrategy** - Rebuilds solution from ruin (recreate/insertion operators)
- **SearchStrategy** - Combines ruin + insertion with acceptance criteria
- **SolutionAcceptor** - Accepts/rejects solutions (e.g., SchrimpfAcceptance)
- **SolutionSelector** - Selects next strategy (e.g., SelectBest, WeightedOperatorSelector)

#### Base Classes (Abstract):
- **AbstractVehicle** - Handles vehicle indices, user data
- **AbstractJob** - Handles job indices, user data
- **AbstractRuinStrategy** - Base for ruin strategies
- **AbstractInsertionStrategy** - Base for insertion strategies
- **AbstractInsertionCalculator** - Cost calculation for insertions

#### Constraint Framework:
- **Constraint** - Base marker interface
- **HardActivityConstraint** - Activity-level hard constraints
- **HardRouteConstraint** - Route-level hard constraints
- **SoftActivityConstraint** - Activity-level soft constraints (penalties)
- **SoftRouteConstraint** - Route-level soft constraints
- **ConstraintManager** - Manages all constraints

#### Cost Framework:
- **VehicleRoutingTransportCosts** - Distance/time costs between locations
- **VehicleRoutingActivityCosts** - Activity costs (e.g., waiting time)
- **SolutionCostCalculator** - Total solution cost calculation

#### Listener/Observer Pattern:
Multiple listener types for monitoring algorithm execution:
- **VehicleRoutingAlgorithmListener** - Overall algorithm events
- **AlgorithmEventListener** - New event system with specific event types
- **RuinListener**, **InsertionListener** - Strategy-specific listeners
- Event types: AlgorithmStarts, IterationStarts, IterationEnds, AlgorithmEnds, StrategyExecuted, etc.

### 4. Java Coding Style Conventions

From `.editorconfig` and code examination:

- **Indentation**: 4 spaces (no tabs)
- **Line Length**: Max 100 characters
- **Charset**: UTF-8
- **Line Endings**: LF
- **Continuation Indent**: 8 spaces
- **Formatting Tags**: `@formatter:off` / `@formatter:on` supported
- **File Endings**: Final newline required

**Code Patterns Observed**:
- Apache License header on all files
- Javadoc on public interfaces/classes with parameter descriptions
- Builder pattern extensively used (e.g., `VehicleImpl.Builder`, `Service.Builder`, `VehicleRoutingProblem.Builder`)
- Interface segregation with small, focused interfaces
- Default methods in interfaces for backward compatibility (Java 8+)
- Listener/Observer pattern throughout
- Factory methods and factory classes (e.g., `RuinOperatorFactory`, `InsertionOperatorFactory`)
- Enum usage for type-safe options (e.g., `FleetSize`, `Job.Type`)

### 5. Package Structure

**Core Packages** (`com.graphhopper.jsprit.core`):

```
algorithm/
  ├── acceptor/              (Solution acceptance strategies)
  ├── box/                   (Jsprit factory & builders)
  ├── listener/              (Algorithm listeners & events)
  ├── listener/events/       (Event types)
  ├── module/                (Algorithm modules: RuinAndRecreate)
  ├── recreate/              (Insertion/recreation strategies)
  ├── recreate/listener/     (Insertion listeners)
  ├── ruin/                  (Ruin/destroy strategies)
  ├── ruin/distance/         (Distance calculations for ruin)
  ├── ruin/listener/         (Ruin listeners)
  ├── selector/              (Strategy selectors)
  ├── state/                 (State management)
  └── termination/           (Early stopping criteria)

problem/
  ├── constraint/            (Hard/soft constraints)
  ├── cost/                  (Transport & activity costs)
  ├── driver/                (Driver models)
  ├── job/                   (Job types: Service, Shipment, Pickup, Delivery, Break)
  ├── misc/                  (Misc: Skills, Capacity, Location)
  ├── solution/              (Solutions container)
  ├── solution/route/        (Vehicle routes)
  ├── solution/route/activity/ (Tour activities)
  ├── solution/route/state/  (Route state)
  ├── solution/spec/         (Solution specifications for initial solutions)
  └── vehicle/               (Vehicle types & implementations)

analysis/                     (Solution analysis)
reporting/                    (Reporting utilities)
util/                         (Utilities)

jsprit-analysis/
  └── analysis/toolbox/      (Visualization & analysis tools)
```

### 6. Existing Documentation & Configuration

**Documentation**:
- `/docs/` - Multiple markdown guides:
  - Getting-Started.md
  - Simple-Example.md
  - Walkthrough---Algorithm.md
  - Walkthrough Constraints.md
  - Classical-Problems-Examples.md
  - VRP-with-backhauls-example.md
  - Heterogeneous-Fleet.md
  - Dial-a-ride-problem.md
  - Other-Projects.md
  - Acknowledgement.md

**Configuration Files**:
- `README.md` - Project overview with quick start
- `CHANGELOG.md` - Detailed change history
- `MIGRATION.md` - Migration guide from v1.x to v2.0
- `WHATS_NEW.md` - Version history and features
- `NOTICE.md` - Dependencies information
- `LICENSE.md` - Apache 2.0 license
- `.editorconfig` - IDE/editor formatting rules
- `.gitignore` - Git ignore patterns
- `.github/` - GitHub workflows

**No CLAUDE.md or .claude/ folder exists** - documentation is standard open-source format.

### 7. Build System & Tooling

**Maven Configuration**:
- **Parent POM**: Manages versions and common dependencies
- **Plugins**:
  - maven-compiler-plugin (Java 21)
  - maven-surefire-plugin (unit tests)
  - maven-failsafe-plugin (integration tests)
  - maven-javadoc-plugin (documentation)
  - maven-enforcer-plugin (Maven 3.6.0+)
  - maven-gpg-plugin (release signing)
  - central-publishing-maven-plugin (central repo publishing)

**Dependencies**:
- **trove4j** (3.0.3) - High-performance collections
- **commons-math3** (3.6.1) - Math utilities
- **slf4j-api** (2.0.16) - Logging facade
- **JUnit 5** (5.10.2) - Testing framework
- **Mockito** (5.10.0) - Mocking library

**Memory Settings**: Tests run with `-Xmx100m -Xms100m`

### 8. Key Entry Points

- **`Jsprit.java`** (`com.graphhopper.jsprit.core.algorithm.box`) - Main factory for creating algorithms
- **`VehicleRoutingProblem.Builder`** - Fluent builder for problem construction
- **`VehicleRoutingAlgorithm.searchSolutions()`** - Main solve method
- **`Solutions.bestOf()`** - Utility for solution selection

### 9. File Statistics

- **jsprit-core**: 344 main source files + 137 test files
- **jsprit-analysis**: 9 source files (visualization, analysis tools)
- **Total**: ~500 Java files across the project

This is a well-architected, mature library with clear separation of concerns, extensive use of design patterns (Builder, Factory, Observer, Strategy), and comprehensive constraint/cost modeling for complex VRP variants.
